calendar.matters:
Petition For Order Comfirming Successor Trustee And Trust Assets
Off Calendar Oct-01-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: David Yang for and with (petitioner) Harry Ashton.

The Court stated that we are back looking for more information regarding entitlement to income of and if the deed of trust is a trust asset and that it was found that the income went into an account owned by the trustors and Mr. Ashton. Mr. Yang reported that they produced the tax return of the deceased, that they identified interest from the promissory note and deed of trust and that there is a general assignment in the trust. The Court stated that it is not sure this is enough. Mr. Yang stated that up until 2007 the money went into a personal account of the decedents, that after 2007 the title was changed to add the son, that the account was set up as a joint account however the decedent had sole control. The Court stated that the issue is whether he was taking the payments personally or as the trust, how was it treated and that the evidence seems to be that he had control of it. Mr. Yang stated that there are declarations from the children indicating that it was in the trust. The Court stated that wishing it was in the trust does not satisfy Heggstad. Mr. Yang stated that the trustor had 5 children, that the money was reported on his taxes and that the totality of evidence is that it was a trust asset. The Court stated that he was handling it himself, it was not in a trust account and asked why is that not evidence that it was not a trust asset. Mr. Yang stated that the decedent was not familiar with the way a trust works and that they did not properly title the account. Mr. Ashton stated that his name was put on the account after his mother died. The Court stated that this is not consistent with a trust, that it will confirm Mr. Ashton as trustee but may or may not find that the account is a trust asset. The petition for order confirming successor trustee and trust assets was taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputies Watts & Siu. (Not Reported)

Petition For Order Comfirming Successor Trustee And Trust Assets
Off Calendar Sep-08-2015 Continued To Sep-30-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Comfirming Successor Trustee And Trust Assets
Off Calendar Aug-18-2015 Continued To Sep-08-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: David Yang for and with (petitioner) Harry Ashton.

The Court asked why the bank is concerned with how title is held and why they are creating an issue here. Mr. Yang stated that they have spoken with the bank, that the title officer will not authorize the loan and that the bank recommended the wording in the order. The Court stated that the deed of trust is in the name of individuals, that the payments are in their name, that it was not put into the trust, that there was a general assignment, asked if the account was in existence in 1990 when the general assignment was made and stated that it needs some evidence in support that the payments were meant to be trust assets. Mr. Yang stated that there is no schedule A, that they are relying on the general assignment and that he understands the courts concern. Mr. Ashton stated that he has the check registers, believes his father was always with this bank and agreed to check the information he has to confirm the date. The Court stated that it will continue the matter for counsel to supply supplemental evidence and to speak to the bank to clarify the issue of how title/accounts are held. The Petition for order confirming successor trustee and trust assets was continued to September 8, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)
